samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i is used to represent the current bit position of the divisor. The parameter j is used to represent the current bit position of the dividend. The parameter rem_temp is used to represent the current remainder.

Divide the 16-bit dividend A by the 8-bit divisor B to obtain the quotient and remainder.

First, initialize two registers a_reg and b_reg with the values of A and B, respectively.

Then, in a combinational always block, compare the higher bits of the dividend with the divisor:

If the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1 and update the remainder.

Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed.

Note that the quotient and remainder should be updated in a first always block.
